volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: The Captives (Section 4.2.857b - Section 4.2.900b)

sections.label: Section 4.2.857b
sections.translation: Yes , you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.857c
sections.original: Tum tu mi igitur erus es .
sections.translation: Then you are my master .

sections.label: Section 4.2.857d
sections.translation: Yes , and a kindly disposed one . Do you wish me to make you happy ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.858b
sections.translation: Certainly I would , rather than miserable .

sections.label: Section 4.2.859
sections.translation: Give me your hand .

sections.label: Section 4.2.859b
sections.translation: Here is my hand .

sections.label: Section 4.2.859c
sections.translation: All the Gods are blessing you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.859d
sections.translation: I don ’ t feel it so .

sections.label: Section 4.2.860
sections.original: Non enim es in senticeto , eo non sentis . sed iube
sections.translation: Why , you are not in a quickset hedge [*] , therefore you don ’ t feel it ; but order the vessels , in a clean state , to be got for you forthwith in readiness for the sacrifice , and one lamb to be brought here with all haste , a fat one .

sections.label: Section 4.2.862b
sections.translation: Why ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.862c
sections.translation: That you may offer sacrifice

sections.label: Section 4.2.863
sections.translation: To which one of the Gods ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.863b
sections.original: Mi hercle , nam égo nunc tibi sum summus Iuppiter ,
sections.translation: To myself , i ’ faith , for now am I your supreme Jupiter . I likewise am your salvation , your fortune , your life , your delight , your joy .

sections.label: Section 4.2.865
sections.original: proin tu deum hunc saturitate facias tranquillum tibi .
sections.translation: Do you at once , then , make this Divinity propitious to you by cramming him .

sections.label: Section 4.2.866
sections.translation: You seem to me to be hungry .

sections.label: Section 4.2.866b
sections.translation: For myself am I hungry , and not for you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.867
sections.translation: I readily allow of it at your own good will .

sections.label: Section 4.2.867b
sections.translation: I believe you ; from a boy [*] you were in the habit —

sections.label: Section 4.2.868
sections.translation: May Jupiter and the Gods confound you .

sections.label: Section 4.2.868b
sections.original: Te hercle — mi aequom est gratias
sections.translation: I ’ troth , ‘ tis fair that for my news you should return me thanks ; such great happiness do I now bring you from the harbour .

sections.label: Section 4.2.870
sections.translation: Now you are flattering me .

sections.label: Section 4.2.870b
sections.original: Abi , stultu'sstultus es , sero post tempus venis .
sections.translation: Begone , you simpleton ; you have arrived behind time , too late .

sections.label: Section 4.2.871
sections.original: Igitur olim si advenissem , magis tu tum istuc diceres ;
sections.translation: If I had come sooner , then for that reason you might rather have said that . Now , receive this joyous news of me which I bring you ; for at the harbour I just now saw your son Philopolemus in the common fly-boat , alive , safe and sound , and likewise there that other young man together with him ,

sections.label: Section 4.2.875
sections.original: Aleum una et tuom Stalagmum servom , qui aufugit domo ,
sections.translation: and Stalagmus your slave , who fled from your house , who stole from you your little son , the child of four years old .

sections.label: Section 4.2.877
sections.original: Abi in malam rem , ludis me .
sections.translation: Away with you to utter perdition ! You are trifling with me .

sections.label: Section 4.2.877b
sections.translation: So may holy Gluttony [*] love me , Hegio , and so may she ever dignify me with her name , I did see —

sections.label: Section 4.2.879b
sections.translation: My son ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.879c
sections.translation: Your son , and my good Genius .

sections.label: Section 4.2.880
sections.translation: That Elean captive , too ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.880b
sections.translation: Yes , by Apollo [*]

sections.label: Section 4.2.880c
sections.translation: The slave , too ? My slave Stalagmus , he that stole my son —?

sections.label: Section 4.2.881b
sections.translation: Yes , by Cora .

sections.label: Section 4.2.882
sections.translation: So long a time ago ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.882b
sections.translation: Yes , by Praeneste !

sections.label: Section 4.2.882c
sections.translation: Is he arrived ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.882d
sections.translation: Yes , by Signia !

sections.label: Section 4.2.883
sections.translation: For sure ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.883b
sections.translation: Yes , by Phrysinone !

sections.label: Section 4.2.883c
sections.translation: Have a care , if you please .

sections.label: Section 4.2.883d
sections.translation: Yes , by Alatrium !

sections.label: Section 4.2.884
sections.original: Quid tu per barbaricas urbes iuras ?
sections.translation: Why are you swearing by foreign cities ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.884b
sections.translation: Why , because they are just as disagreable as you were declaring your fare to be .

sections.label: Section 4.2.885b
sections.translation: Woe be to you !

sections.label: Section 4.2.886
sections.original: Quippe quando mihi nil credis , quod ego dico sedulo .
sections.translation: Because that you don ’ t believe me at all in what I say in sober earnestness . But of what country was Stalagmus , at the time when ne departed hence ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.888
sections.translation: A Sicilian .

sections.label: Section 4.2.888b
sections.original: At nunc Siculus non est , Boius est , Boiam terit :
sections.translation: But now he is not a Sicilian — he is a Boian ; he has got a Boian woman . [*] A wife , I suppose , has been given to him for the sake of obtaining children .

sections.label: Section 4.2.890
sections.original: Dic , bonan fide tu mi istaec verba dixisti ?
sections.translation: Tell me , have you said these words to me in good earnest ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.890b
sections.translation: In good earnest .

sections.label: Section 4.2.891
sections.original: Di immortales , iterum gnatus videor , si vera autumas .
sections.translation: Immortal Gods , I seem to be born again , if you are telling the truth .

sections.label: Section 4.2.892
sections.original: Ain tu ? dubium habebis etiam , sancte quom ego iurem tibi ?
sections.translation: Do you say so ? Will you still entertain doubts , when I have solemnly sworn to you ? In fine , Hegio , if you have little confidence in my oath , go yourself to the harbour and see .

sections.label: Section 4.2.894b
sections.original: Facere certumst . tu intus cura quod opus est .
sections.translation: I ’ m determined to do so . Do you arrange in-doors what ’ s requisite . Use , ask for , take from my larder what you like ; I appoint you cellarman .

sections.label: Section 4.2.896
sections.original: Nam hercle , nisi mantiscinatus probe ero , fusti pectito .
sections.translation: Now , by my troth , if I have not prophesied truly to you , do you comb me out with a cudgel .

sections.label: Section 4.2.897
sections.original: Aeternum tibi dapinabo victum , si vera autumas .
sections.translation: I ’ ll find you in victuals to the end , if you are telling me the truth .

sections.label: Section 4.2.898
sections.translation: Whence shall it be ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.898b
sections.translation: From myself and from my son .

sections.label: Section 4.2.898c
sections.translation: Do you promise that ?

sections.label: Section 4.2.898d
sections.translation: I do promise it .

sections.label: Section 4.2.899
sections.original: At ego tuom tibi advenisse filium respondeo .
sections.translation: But I , in return , promise [*] you that your son has arrived .

sections.label: Section 4.2.900
sections.translation: Manage as well as ever you can .

sections.label: Section 4.2.900b
sections.translation: A happy walk there to you , and a happy walk back .
